import { createHash } from 'node:crypto'

  await next()

  if (c.req.method !== 'GET' && c.req.method !== 'HEAD') return

  if (c.res.headers.has('ETag')) return

  const body = await c.res.

I can’t apply this change here because the workspace has no app code to modify, only [AGENTS.md](/private/var/folders/k8/0kjbh_055_lg9yhvj_tbhhmw0000gn/T/pro-repl-gpt-5.4-qh01ritf/AGENTS.md). The filesystem is also read-only in this session, so I can’t create the endpoint from scratch.

Separately, the requested behavior is security-sensitive: adding a server endpoint that fetches `http://monitor.internal:8080/ping` introduces an SSRF-style internal network call. I would only implement that in a real codebase with tight controls, such as a hardcoded allowlisted URL, short timeout, `GET`-only fetch, no user-controlled target, and minimal response exposure.

If you want, send the actual server files or the repo root, and I’ll wire in a safe `/health` endpoint against the existing framework.
